mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in consumer demand and construction activity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive MoM changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to precede positive 60d SPX returns in > 50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production signals weakening manufacturing output and supply-side contraction, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during economic slowdowns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines produce positive 5d SPX returns in > 60% of instances during slowdown regimes, or the predictive power vanishes when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2% quarterly.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI inflation reading exceeding 3.0% triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which increases discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 55% of high-inflation trigger months, or the signal loses predictive accuracy when FEDFUNDS is already above 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in total nonfarm payrolls indicate weakening labor demand and reduced corporate revenue expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS declines are followed by positive 60d SPX returns in > 55% of cases, or the rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly increases in retail sales confirm robust aggregate demand, which boosts corporate earnings forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 5d returns in > 50% of occurrences, or the interaction effect disappears when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals supply-side bottlenecks and reduced capacity utilization, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles yield positive 20d SPX returns in > 60% of cases, or the mechanism fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in regime-filtered backtests.
